candidate_sources_rejected: adjacent respiratory guidance generalized into a complete asthma follow-up pathway
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Only the reviewed exact domains apply; remaining asthma follow-up content lacks exact support.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with relevant asthma follow-up history, findings and clinician decisions entered.
setting_applicability: Partial for Dubai telehealth and adult asthma follow-up; diagnosis, treatment and escalation remain clinician responsibilities.
UAE_applicability: DHA is explicitly applicable to Dubai telehealth asthma care; NICE requires UAE adaptation.
recency_verification: The cited official documents and exact registered sections were reviewed on 2026-07-18.
superseded_check: The registered official-source status was checked; no newer replacement was identified in the source record.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports asthma symptom and diagnostic context for asthma follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-assessed warning prompts for asthma follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports qualified objective-test review for asthma follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports asthma-control monitoring and review for asthma follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports inhaler technique, adherence and action plans for asthma follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

unresolved_source_gaps: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Interval symptoms, attacks, reliever use, night waking, function, triggers, adherence, technique, objective results, examination, treatment and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for asthma follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
